title: trapa japonica var. jeholensis
intro: Trapa japonica var. jeholensis, also known as water chestnut, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Trapaceae. It is a submerged aquatic plant that grows in freshwater lakes, ponds, and rivers, and is native to Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, headache, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, and to relieve symptoms of various ailments.
